options.why: Read the vertical distance as the first coordinate, swapping $x$ and $y$.
Ignored the half-gridlines and rounded each coordinate toward the origin.
Read the point as above the origin instead of below, giving the wrong $y$-sign.

questions.solution_text:
$F$ is $2.5$ units left and $1.5$ units down.
$(-2.5, -1.5)$

questions.question_text: To plot the point $(-1.5, 3)$, start at the origin and move:

options.why: Used the $y$-coordinate for the horizontal move, reading the pair in the wrong order.
Treated the negative $x$-coordinate as a move to the right.
Treated the positive $y$-coordinate as a downward move.

questions.solution_text:
$x = -1.5$ means $1.5$ left; $y = 3$ means $3$ up.
$1.5$ left, then $3$ up

options.why: Swapped the pair; a point on the $x$-axis has $y = 0$, not $x = 0$.
Rounded the half-gridline position to the nearest whole unit.
Counted the $x$-axis itself as one unit up instead of using $y = 0$.

questions.solution_text:
$K$ is on the $x$-axis, so $y = 0$; its $x$-value is halfway between $2$ and $3$.
$(2.5, 0)$

options.text: They have the same $x$-coordinate, so they lie on the same vertical line.
They have the same $y$-coordinate, so they lie on the same horizontal line.
options.why: Confused the axes; a shared $x$-value gives a vertical line, not a horizontal one.

options.text: They are reflections of each other across the $x$-axis.
options.why: Reflection across the $x$-axis needs opposite $y$-values of equal size, but $1$ and $-2$ are not opposites.
Both points are at $x = -3$, well left of the $y$-axis, not on it.

questions.solution_text:
$P = (-3, 1)$ and $Q = (-3, -2)$; both have $x = -3$.
They have the same $x$-coordinate, so they lie on the same vertical line.

options.why: Swapped the two coordinates of the fourth vertex.

questions.question_text: A point lies $3$ units left of the origin and $4$ units up. Which coordinates are correct?

options.why: Wrote the vertical distance first and horizontal second, swapping the pair and its signs.
Recorded 'left' as a positive $x$-coordinate instead of negative.
Swapped the two values while keeping the horizontal sign negative.

questions.solution_text:
Left of the origin gives $x = -3$; up gives $y = 4$.
$(-3, 4)$
